name: LangChain
tagline: Building applications with LLMs through composability
description: LangChain is a framework for developing applications powered by language models. It enables applications that are context-aware and can reason about how to answer based on provided context. The framework provides modular components for working with language models and off-the-shelf chains for common use cases.
usage_examples:
from langchain.llms import OpenAI
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate

llm = OpenAI(temperature=0.9)
prompt = PromptTemplate(
    input_variables=["product"],
    template="What is a good name for a company that makes {product}?",
)

from langchain.chains import LLMChain
chain = LLMChain(llm=llm, prompt=prompt)
print(chain.run("colorful socks"))

code_examples.code:
from langchain.llms import OpenAI
llm = OpenAI(temperature=0.9)
text = "What would be a good company name for a company that makes colorful socks?"
print(llm(text))

search_text: langchain building applications with llms through composability llm langchain gpt ai chatbot openai agents rag modular components for llm applications pre-built chains for common patterns memory management for conversations

experiences.review: Excellent framework for building LLM applications. The modular design makes it easy to swap components and experiment with different approaches.
experiences.tips: Start with the quickstart guide. Use LangSmith for debugging. Consider using LCEL (LangChain Expression Language) for better composability.
